lastCommit.message: feat: implement PostgreSQL Schema and Migration Foundation (attempt 3)

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: PostgreSQL Schema and Migration Foundation in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The workspace changes for F002 are confined to migration foundation assets (`migrations/*`, `scripts/check-sqlx-migrations.sh`, `scripts/setup-dev.sh`, workflow/docs/tooling), with no backend handler/OpenAPI/generated-client delta, so the active contract-sync policy is satisfied for this feature. The implementation uses `sqlx migrate` lifecycle commands, pins PostgreSQL 16, enforces UUID global PK (`global_pk`), BIGINT minor units (`money_minor`), enum types, and append-only guards for `audit_event`/`payroll_ledger_entry`, and adds an up/down verifier plus CI gate. I could not execute the Docker-backed verifier in this sandbox due denied Docker socket access, so runtime validation here is based on static review of the scripts/migrations/workflow.

reinforcementResults.name: Persistence Integrity Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No blocking issues found for this feature in the current workspace. The implementation is migration-driven with `sqlx migrate` (`migrations/`, `package.json` scripts), targets PostgreSQL 16, enforces a single global UUID PK standard (`global_pk`), BIGINT minor-unit money (`money_minor`), enum-backed states, UTC `TIMESTAMPTZ` `_utc` columns, and append-only protections on event/ledger tables with trigger guards. Real-DB verification is implemented in `scripts/check-sqlx-migrations.sh` and wired into CI via `.github/workflows/postgresql-migration-foundation.yml`. Local execution of the verifier was blocked here by Docker sandbox permissions, so runtime proof is via the committed verification gate.

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass for this feature scope. The workspace includes a single `sqlx migrate` migration pair with audited relational schema, UUID-based global PK convention (`global_pk`), BIGINT minor-unit money domain (`money_minor`), enum-backed state types, UTC `TIMESTAMPTZ` columns, and append-only trigger guards for `audit_event`/`payroll_ledger_entry`. Migration lifecycle wiring is present via `sqlx migrate run/revert` scripts, startup migration execution, and a dedicated CI workflow invoking `scripts/check-sqlx-migrations.sh` for clean-DB up/down plus invariant assertions. I could not execute the Docker-based verifier in this sandbox due Docker socket restrictions, so runtime execution proof is inferred from the committed verifier + CI gate. Post-cutover in-memory persistence removal is a later-phase concern and not a blocker for this foundation feature.

executionConstraints: Use PostgreSQL 16+ with `sqlx migrate` only.
Use one global PK standard after `ISS-006` decision.
Use UTC timestamps + BIGINT minor units.

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Authentication Middleware (Mock-Only Phase Scope) (attempt 3)

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Authentication Middleware (Mock-Only Phase Scope) in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Reviewed against F05 scope and policy: auth is centralized in SvelteKit hooks (`event.locals` injection + role/scope guards), mock-only CLAR-002 enforcement is explicit (`AUTH_PROVIDER` hard-gated to `mock`), and expiry/refresh/tamper paths are handled in runtime/provider logic. Mock auth flows for employee/vendor/admin are implemented and covered by tests. I validated behavior with `node --import tsx --test tests/runtime.test.ts tests/hooks.test.ts` (9/9 passing). No backend handler/OpenAPI/generated-client files changed in this feature branch, so the contract-sync policy is satisfied for this scope.

reinforcementResults.name: Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No current-scope blockers found. Auth is centralized in SvelteKit middleware: actor context injection plus role/permission/scope enforcement are in `apps/web/src/hooks.server.ts`; mock-only provider selection and safe session expiry/refresh/tamper handling are in `apps/web/src/lib/server/auth/runtime.ts` and `apps/web/src/lib/server/auth/mock-provider.ts`; dev mock sign-in for employee/vendor/admin is implemented in `apps/web/src/routes/auth/mock/+server.ts`. Evidence checks are covered by passing web auth tests (`node --import tsx --test tests/**/*.test.ts`) in `apps/web/tests/hooks.test.ts` and `apps/web/tests/runtime.test.ts`.

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No blockers found for this feature scope. The SvelteKit `handle` hook centralizes authn/authz, injects actor context (`locals.auth`/`locals.actor`), enforces role and scoped-path guards, and clears invalid sessions. The auth runtime keeps a provider seam (`AuthProvider`) while enforcing CLAR-002 mock-only mode (non-mock providers hard-fail), and safely handles expiry/refresh/tamper cases in the mock provider. Dev mock auth supports employee/vendor/admin. I also verified no ad hoc page-level auth checks or real-provider coupling in the web feature surface. Validation: `cd apps/web && pnpm test` passed (9/9). `pnpm check` could not run in this read-only sandbox because SvelteKit needs to write `.svelte-kit` files.

executionConstraints: Respect `CLAR-002` mock-auth-only scope.
Keep provider seam swappable for later real auth.
Centralize authn/authz in hooks/middleware.

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Runtime Infrastructure Security and Access Baseline (attempt 3)

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Runtime Infrastructure Security and Access Baseline in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The workspace satisfies the Runtime Infrastructure Security and Access Baseline for this feature: PostgreSQL topology plus PgBouncer RW/RO services are declared with transaction pool mode; runtime deployments are wired to pooled `DATABASE_RW_URL`/`DATABASE_RO_URL` endpoints (no legacy `DATABASE_URL` in Kubernetes runtime deployments); Gateway + TLS/CORS/request-size/rate-limit policies, default-deny network policy with scoped allow rules, and ExternalSecret-based secret externalization with dual-key rotation wiring are declared; and SvelteKit adapter-node frontend Deployment/Service plus cache-control behavior are declared and covered by feature tests. The active API-contract policy is satisfied for this feature because no API contract boundary artifacts were changed. Test execution was attempted but blocked by the read-only sandbox (`target/debug/.cargo-lock` permission).

reinforcementResults.name: Canonical File Storage Gate
reinforcementResults.summary:
Pass. The workspace declares and enforces the runtime infra/security baseline for this feature: PostgreSQL topology with separate PgBouncer RW/RO transaction pools (`ops/kubernetes/base/postgres-topology.yaml`, `ops/kubernetes/base/pgbouncer.yaml`), runtime RW/RO DB endpoint wiring via externalized secrets in all runtime deployments (`deployment*.yaml` + `external-secrets.yaml`), gateway TLS/CORS/request-size/rate-limit policies (`ops/kubernetes/base/gateway.yaml`), default-deny plus scoped network isolation (`networkpolicy-default-deny.yaml`, `networkpolicy-runtime-allow.yaml`), and SvelteKit adapter-node frontend Deployment/Service plus cache-control contract (`deployment-web.yaml`, `service-web.yaml`, `apps/web/src/hooks.server.ts`, `apps/web/tests/hooks.test.ts`).

Active object-storage policy checks are also satisfied in-repo: MIME/size validation is enforced in the upload pipeline before artifact acceptance (`src/object_storage.rs`, object-storage upload handler tests in `src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs`), artifact persistence uses managed `s3://` references rather than DB binary/base64 payload paths (`src/vendor_compliance.rs`, `src/menu_supply_window.rs`, `src/vendor_fulfillment.rs`), and bucket access provisioning is explicitly restricted (`ops/kubernetes/base/job-object-storage-provision.yaml` sets anonymous access to `none`).

Note: I could not execute `cargo test --test runtime_infrastructure_security_baseline` in this environment because the workspace is read-only and Cargo could not create `target/debug/.cargo-lock`.

reinforcementResults.name: Runtime Security Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The runtime infrastructure security/access baseline is satisfied in this workspace: PostgreSQL topology plus PgBouncer RW/RO Deployments+Services are declared and test-covered; runtime Deployments are wired to `DATABASE_RW_URL`/`DATABASE_RO_URL` (with legacy `DATABASE_URL` excluded); Gateway manifests include TLS-only listener plus CORS, request-size, and rate-limit controls; default-deny and allowlist NetworkPolicies are present; credentials are sourced via ExternalSecret (no plaintext Kubernetes Secret manifests in the baseline) with dual-key rotation entries where required; and SvelteKit adapter-node frontend Deployment/Service plus cache-control contract are declared and validated.

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The workspace satisfies this feature’s active policy and acceptance signals: `ops/kubernetes/base/postgres-topology.yaml` + `pgbouncer.yaml` declare CNPG topology and separate RW/RO PgBouncer services in transaction mode; runtime deployments use `DATABASE_RW_URL`/`DATABASE_RO_URL` (no legacy `DATABASE_URL`) with network policies enforcing runtime-to-PgBouncer isolation and default-deny (`networkpolicy-default-deny.yaml`, `networkpolicy-runtime-allow.yaml`); edge controls enforce HTTPS/TLS, CORS, request-size, and rate limiting (`gateway.yaml`); secrets are externalized with dual-key rotation wiring (`external-secrets.yaml`); and the SvelteKit adapter-node frontend deployment/service and cache-control contract are declared (`deployment-web.yaml`, `service-web.yaml`, `apps/web/src/hooks.server.ts`) and covered by `tests/runtime_infrastructure_security_baseline.rs`.

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The feature is implemented behind channel-level flags and user preferences, with per-channel retry/statistics/failure isolation and idempotent channel handling in the dispatcher. API-surface changes for rush-reminder preferences are synchronized across backend contract source (`src/contract.rs`), published OpenAPI artifacts (`contract/openapi/openapi.yaml` and `contract/openapi/openapi.json`), generated TS client artifacts (`contract/generated/ts-client/...`), and contract test coverage (`tests/openapi_contract_platform.rs`).

reinforcementResults.name: Cache and Event Correctness Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Feature review passes for current scope. Optional channel expansion is implemented behind feature flags and preferences (`PRELAUNCH_RUSH_REMINDER_EMAIL_CHANNEL_ENABLED`, `PRELAUNCH_RUSH_REMINDER_WEB_PUSH_CHANNEL_ENABLED` + persisted per-user channel prefs), channels can be enabled independently, and in-app remains the baseline path. Dispatcher behavior is per-channel with independent retry limits, channel-level stats/logging, and per-channel idempotency via handled delivery keys. State mutation is SQL-authoritative for rush reminders (no cache read path introduced for this feature). Existing workspace evidence also covers consumer replay idempotency, DLQ-after-retry behavior, and DLQ alert rule linkage. Note: I could not execute tests in this environment due read-only sandbox write denial on `target/debug/.cargo-lock`, so this verdict is based on static code and test inspection.

reinforcementResults.name: UX Reliability Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No blocking issues found for ISS-004 in the current workspace. The feature is implemented behind flags/preferences with independent email and web-push toggles, while keeping in-app as the baseline channel (`src/rush_reminder.rs`, `src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs`). Dispatch now applies per-channel gating, retry policy, channel-level observability stats, failure isolation, and idempotent per-channel handling. OpenAPI/client contracts were updated for the new channel preference fields and feature-off 404 behavior. Active UX reliability policy evidence is present in existing web code/tests for loading + duplicate-submit suppression, zh-TW offline/API messaging, and employee mobile vs vendor/admin desktop-tablet responsive modes. Test execution was not possible in this read-only sandbox.

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The optional channel expansion is implemented behind explicit feature flags and preferences: runtime/channel flags are parsed and applied (`src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs`), and dispatch requires both `channel_feature_flags.supports(channel)` and `preferences.allows_channel(channel)` (`src/rush_reminder.rs`). In-app remains the baseline path (`InApp` always allowed). Dispatcher behavior is per-channel with independent retry, stats, and failure isolation (`src/rush_reminder.rs` dispatch loop; `src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs` channel dispatch reporting), and idempotency is channel-scoped via `ReminderDeliveryKey` + `handled_registry` persisted in snapshots. Policy evidence checks are satisfied for this feature scope: production boot uses SQL persistence, test-only in-memory paths are `#[cfg(test)]`, and persisted state is JSONB (not DB base64 storage).
